description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white to light gray coloration. Its surface is highly textured, featuring numerous protruding, elongated mineral formations and rough, uneven patches. The rock appears to be composed mainly of a crystalline or sedimentary material, with some areas showing a more compact, solid structure and others displaying clusters of nodular growths. There are visible human-made markings, including black writing on one of the flatter surfaces, suggesting cataloging or identification. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one side more densely covered in mineral protrusions and the opposite side relatively smoother. The rock's rugged appearance and unique surface features make it a notable obstacle or natural barrier in an urban environment.
description: A rough, white-gray rock with dense mineral protrusions and visible cataloging markings. Its surface is highly textured and irregular, making it a distinctive urban obstacle.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's most textured side, densely covered with elongated mineral protrusions and visible black writing on the upper surface.
description_view_1: The rock appears smoother and less detailed from this angle, with fewer visible mineral clusters and a more solid, compact surface.
description_view_2: This side shows a mix of rough and smoother areas, with some mineral formations visible but less pronounced than in view 0.
description_view_3: The top-down perspective reveals the rock's irregular shape and the concentration of mineral protrusions, as well as the cataloging markings on the upper surface.
